name: Triboro Postal
description_short: Triboro Postal FCU is a credit union established in 1935 offering savings, checking, loans, and credit cards with NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000.
description_long: Triboro Postal Federal Credit Union (TPFCU) has been serving members since 1935, positioning itself as an established financial cooperative in the credit union space. The organization operates as a member-owned institution providing comprehensive banking services rather than pursuing profit for shareholders. TPFCU offers a full suite of consumer financial products including deposit accounts, lending services, and credit products to its membership base. The credit union maintains an online presence with digital banking capabilities and mobile app access alongside traditional phone and in-person banking. The institution provides savings accounts (Share and Share Plus), no-fee checking accounts with unlimited check writing, holiday club accounts, certificates of deposit with $1,000 minimums, and retirement savings vehicles including Traditional IRAs, Roth IRAs, and Coverdell ESAs. On the lending side, TPFCU offers personal loans, auto loans with shopping tools, home loans, and home equity lines of credit. The credit union also issues VISA Platinum and Access credit cards. All deposit accounts carry NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000, the standard federal coverage limit for credit unions. Triboro Postal's distinguishing features include its long operational history dating to 1935, zero monthly maintenance fees on savings accounts, no minimum balance requirements for checking accounts, and accessible remote loan processing services. The credit union maintains multiple contact channels including a dedicated debit card support line (866-762-0558), general banking phone line (833-975-0039), online banking portal, and mobile application. The organization explicitly states it is an equal opportunity lender and offers scholarship applications for members' families, suggesting community-focused operations beyond basic banking services. As a traditional credit union, TPFCU appears well-suited for members seeking straightforward banking with low fees and competitive loan options. However, the website provides limited transparency regarding specific interest rates, APRs, loan terms, or detailed product specifications, requiring prospective members to contact the institution directly for pricing information. The organization serves as a stable, established banking option rather than a fintech innovator, appealing to members prioritizing reliability and NCUA protection over digital-first features.
services: Share Savings Accounts with no monthly maintenance fees
Checking Accounts with no minimum balance and unlimited check writing
Time Certificates of Deposit (1-year and 3-year terms)
VISA Debit Card and ATM access
pros: No monthly maintenance fees on savings accounts
No minimum balance requirement to open checking account
Unlimited check writing on checking accounts
Deposit accounts insured up to $250,000 by NCUA
Remote loan processing available to save member time
Low minimum to open savings account ($5.00)
Multiple account types including club accounts, CDs, and IRAs with no setup fees
cons: Website provides no specific interest rates, APRs, or loan terms—all pricing requires direct contact
Limited information about credit card rewards, benefits, or terms
No details provided about membership eligibility criteria or field of membership restrictions
Mobile app and online banking mentioned but no features or reviews available on website
Sparse product documentation limits ability to compare offerings with competitors
best_for: Members seeking a stable, fee-free checking and savings option with NCUA protection
Consumers looking to finance vehicle or home purchases through a cooperative institution
Savers interested in retirement accounts (IRAs) and CDs with straightforward terms
Individuals preferring traditional credit union banking over digital-only neobanks
diagnosis: Triboro Postal FCU is best for members seeking a traditional credit union with low fees, NCUA deposit insurance, and basic to mid-range lending products (auto, home, personal loans). The primary caveat is that the website lacks transparent pricing—interest rates, APRs, and specific loan terms are not disclosed, requiring direct contact for any financial decision-making.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
